---
title: "Lab 2 — Example Results"
subtitle: "Week 7 · {{< var quarter >}}"
page-layout: full
---

[← Back to Lab 2 overview](index.qmd)

Example step-response figures from the three experiments (Base, FeedForward, Tuned) for the speed and yaw-rate channels. Each case has two figures:

- **Timeseries** — setpoint, measured response, and actuator command across the full step window.
- **PID internals** — `Tar` / `Act` with metrics text box (top), and the per-term breakdown (`P`, `I`, `D`, `FF`) plus the actuator command (bottom).

A separate **integrator-windup** demonstration on the Base/yaw run shows what happens when the controller is allowed to hold the step long enough for the I term to wind up.


## Speed Channel 

### Base - Speed

ArduRover stock PID gains, FF off, rate limiters off, throttle baseline off. The reference against which **FeedForward** and **Tuned** are compared.

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![Base / speed — timeseries (setpoint, measured GPS speed, throttle command)](images/base_speed_response.png)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![Base / speed — PID internals (Tar/Act with metrics; P/I/D/FF terms with actuator command)](images/base_speed_pids.png)
:::
::::

### Feedforward - Speed

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![FeedForward / speed — timeseries](images/ff_speed_response.png)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![FeedForward / speed — PID internals](images/ff_speed_pids.png)
:::
::::

### Tuned - Speed

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![Tuned / speed — timeseries](images/tuned_speed_response.png)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![Tuned / speed — PID internals](images/tuned_speed_pids.png)
:::
::::


## Yaw-rate (Steering) Channel

### Base - Yaw-rate

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![Base / yaw — timeseries (setpoint, measured EKF yaw rate, rudder command)](images/base_yaw_response.png)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![Base / yaw — PID internals (Tar/Act with metrics; P/I/D/FF terms with rudder command)](images/base_yaw_pids.png)
:::
::::
### Base - Yaw-rate — integrator windup demonstration

The same Base/yaw configuration as above, but the step input is held long enough for the integrator to wind up. The delayed recovery on the step-back illustrates classic I-term windup.

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![Base / yaw / windup — timeseries with extended step hold](images/base_yaw_windup_response.png)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![Base / yaw / windup — PID internals; note the I term grows for the duration of the step and dominates the recovery transient](images/base_yaw_windup_pids.png)
:::
::::
### Feedforward - Yaw-rate

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![FeedForward / yaw — timeseries](images/ff_yaw_response.png)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![FeedForward / yaw — PID internals](images/ff_yaw_pids.png)
:::
::::

### Tuned - Yaw-rate

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![Tuned / yaw — timeseries](images/tuned_yaw_response.png)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
![Tuned / yaw — PID internals](images/tuned_yaw_pids.png)
:::
::::




## Summary

Tuned PID gains and step-response metrics across the three experiments for both channels. Generated by [`closedloop_assess_main.m`](https://github.com/bsb808/introduction-to-feedback-control/blob/main/book/w07_lab_usv_pid/code/closedloop_assess_main.m); the table content is included from `metrics_summary.md`, regenerated each time the analysis script runs.

{{< include metrics_summary.md >}}
